rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CLAUDIA B. RICHARDSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, paragraph 9 of the petition misstated the amount of fees paid on the outstanding attorney fees ($5,450 as shown in the accounting, not $4,000) and no filing fee was charged for this petition, so the costs to be reimbursed to the attorney will be $246.50, and the attorney fees to be paid out the remaining funds set aside in the IOLA account will be $701.67, leaving a liability of $3,593.33 in attorney fees for this accounting period. (Counsel might wish to show the fees and costs in a table, showing dates, amounts, and balances, instead of narrating the history.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY EDA DATI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/09/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The petition requests distribution to the estate of Michael Reichling. A personal representative must be named. Please explain who is the personal representative for the estate of Michael Reichling and file a copy of certified letters. See LRSF 14.35 G. If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. 2) 15 days' required notice must be given to the personal representative of the estate of Michael Reichling. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/25/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIKO ONODERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/04/2022 to be heard by Judge Quinn. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 2) Petitioner needs to prove by admissible evidence that either the presumption in Probate Code 6124 doesn't apply or the presumption needs to be rebutted. 3) Please give 15 days notice on the required judicial council form DE-121. Please include the required language found in the 10th amended general order of the probate and mental health division found on the court's website. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL WALTON GONZALEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/9/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the statutory compensation calculation. The petition alleges the final I&A was $1,507,300.00 The Final I&A alleged $1,507,000.00. It appears this must be amended. 2) Please explain the amount of cash on hand. Petition alleges the petitioner has taken no action to invest cash on hand or to sell equities owned by the estate. Please address the administrator's duty to keep cash in an account that must earn interest. 3) Prayer #1 must be amended. The petition requests the residual funds obtained from the sale be distributed to the sole beneficiary. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.] 4) Prayer #2 must be amended. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/25/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether all services provided were "in connection with and to facilitate the appointment of a conservator, and costs incurred in connection therewith". 2) Petitioners request fees for services in connection with a trust and a restraining order. It is also noted that timesheets attached for these services are heavily redacted. 3) Costs of $6,945.92 were incurred for "Cannon Street, Inc." for private investigation services and service of process. 4) Deborah Fox, attorney for conservator, and Virginia Palmer, attorney for Conservatee, have only been given emailed notice. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSE MARY WILLIAMS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Restatement, Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee; And Complaint For Elder Financial Abuse And Tenant Harassment
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/6/22 for lack of response. It does not appear that proper notice has been given for the amended petition filed on 5/3/21. Although the Examiner substituting for the Examiner assigned to this matter has not seen the courtesy copies of the petitions, a cursory review of them online indicates that neither listed the beneficiaries of the trust and their last know addresses, as a petition concerning a trust is required to by CRC 7.902. If the issues are not resolved at least 10 days prior to 6/6/22, an appearance may be required on 6/6/22 for the Court to determine if the petitions should be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. GREEN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine whether to take the matter off calendar for failure to respond to Examiner notes after 4 continuances. Examiner notes: a. Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined prior to the petition. Please explain and provide the legal basis /cite probate code sections for why San Francisco is the proper venue when the trustee appears to live in Edgewood, MD. b. Please file evidence showing the Wells Fargo bank account ending in 9287 was in existence prior to execution of the trust. c. Please file a current account statement of the bank account. d. Please explain all the Decedent's heirs so the court can determine whether notice is proper under Prob. C. 851 (b). e. The proposed order does not match what is requested in the prayer/request in the petition. It appears the petition must be amended to match what is requested in the proposed order as the prayer/request in the petition does not indicate the account number or where the bank account is and just says "Directing that the Bank Account is an asset of the William H. Green Revocable Trust." Also the second page of the proposed order "That the parties shall execute all documents and perform such additional acts as may be necessary to carry out the above" is not in the petition's prayer/request. f. The trust appears to have been executed on 02/08/2017. The notary page shows 02/08/2018. Will appears to have been executed 02/08/2018. Please explain.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE YANG SU CHO 2015 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Beneficiary'S Petition To Remove The Trustee And Order Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code 17200
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/16/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please provide the legal basis and cite probate code sections explaining how San Francisco is the proper venue. 1 conclusory statement is not adequate. 2) Please address CRC 7.52 and the efforts made to locate and give notice to both Kyung Ja Kim and Eun Tae Kim. It appears there is an Los Angeles address on Decedent's death certificate for the current trustee. 3) Petitioner does not nominate or explain who should be the successor trustee. There is no acceptance by a proposed trustee. The Court will require a bond of all successor trustees not named in the trust instrument unless all beneficiaries waive bond. The assets on hand must be listed with the fair market value to allow the Court to set the bond. [LRSF 14.16 G.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/2/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER MORTIMER CORNYN REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JANUARY 14, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Find That Real Property Is An Asset Of The Trust Under Probate Code 17200(B)(4), 850(A)(3) & 856
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file the current vesting deed for the real property. 2) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries, including all persons in being who may or will receive income or corpus of the trust. (CRC 7.902). 3) Notice must be re-done and include the required language found in the 10th amended general order found on the court's website. Also please give notice of the petition to Florence F. Chan, Jennifer Sullivan, Christian Sullivan, Karen Rose, Kylegh King, William Cornyn, Law Offices of Darcey L. Wong, P.C. [Probate Code 1202]. 4) Please explain whether Madison Roche and/or Nicolas Roche are minors or adults. If they are minors please provide the legal basis / legal authority for whether a GAL needs to be appointed. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FRANCISCO BASANES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/09/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file form DE-147 that includes all required information including the proposed personal representative's phone number. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Please must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/25/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
